text: Between 10:41:02 and 10:41:24, host web01 experienced a rapid SSH brute-force attack from IP 203.0.113.77, with 8 failed login attempts targeting usernames including "root", "test", "ubuntu", "admin", "devops", "oracle". The attacker cycled through credentials at short intervals, consistent with automated tooling such as Hydra or Medusa (MITRE ATT&CK T1110.001 (Brute Force: Password Guessing)). At 10:42:02, the SSH authentication succeeded for user "devops" from IP 203.0.113.77 on port 50012. This successful login followed the brute-force activity described above, suggesting the attacker eventually guessed or obtained valid credentials. At 10:42:05, user "devops" executed a privileged command via sudo to read /etc/shadow as root. Reading the shadow password file is a classic credential harvesting step following initial access (T1003.008 (OS Credential Dumping: /etc/passwd and /etc/shadow)). Overall, this sequence on host web01 represents a multi-stage attack: initial access via SSH followed by privilege escalation and persistence mechanisms, consistent with a hands-on-keyboard intrusion.
